HGSS.071.0000:
Bravo!\f
A promising youth who is about to start
the journey of archaeology has\r
just arrived!\f
HGSS.071.0001:
What? Just a visitor?
Oh, I see...\r
You got me excited for nothing.
HGSS.071.0002:
My staff here at the Ruins Research
Center is excellent, and the research\r
makes good progress with absolutely\r
no effort on my part.\f
If you have any questions, feel free to
talk to my staff!
HGSS.071.0003:
I managed to unravel quite a lot of the
mystery of UNOWN with your help!\f
Thank you, thank you.
Thank you, again!
HGSS.071.0004:
The ruins are about 1,500 years old.
That’s all we know about them.\f
But it’s not known why they were
built...or by whom.
HGSS.071.0005:
Wow, did you catch an UNOWN?
That’s wonderful!\f
Come to think of it... How come we’ve
suddenly started to see UNOWN?
HGSS.071.0006:
You have to hear this!
This is a big discovery!\f
It was you who solved the stone-panel
puzzle that allowed UNOWN to appear.\f
Those tablets are the key to waking
up UNOWN!\f
Still... Why would anyone make such
a key?\f
HGSS.071.0007:
You have to hear this!
This is a big discovery!\f
If you turn on the radio in the ruins,
you can receive mysterious radio waves!\f
It’s proof that UNOWN communicate
with each other using radio waves!\f
HGSS.071.0008:
You have to hear this!
This is a big discovery!\f
There are as many UNOWN as there are
letters in the alphabet!\f
There have been 26 types confirmed.
Why don’t you collect them all?\f
HGSS.071.0009:
How do you like the UNOWN Report
I gave you?\f
UNOWN are recorded as you catch them.
It is quite useful!
HGSS.071.0010:
You have to hear this!
This is a big discovery!\f
We have figured out that UNOWN are in a
certain order!\r
Let me see your report.\f
HGSS.071.0011:
There!
I put the UNOWN in the correct order!\r
We may be able to tell more about them.\f
HGSS.071.0012:
What?
You found a room with UNOWN symbols?\r
That is a big discovery!\f
We will study them right away.\f
HGSS.071.0013:
You have to hear this!
This is a big discovery!\f
We have found two new UNOWN symbols!\f
It means that...
There must be two more kinds of UNOWN\r
somewhere...\f
HGSS.071.0014:
You have to hear this!
We are learning more and more about the\r
symbols on the floor!\f
...It seems that UNOWN were living at
the ruins with a human clan.\f
It is thought that this clan made the
Pokémon statues underground.\f
The UNOWN were easily scared and
wouldn’t go outside.\f
When more and more people started to
appear around the ruins, the clan that\r
wished to live in harmony with the UNOWN\r
eventually left...\f
Thanks to our research and your
help, we have unraveled so much about\r
their mystery!\f
We have so many things we don’t know
left, of course.\f
Archaeology never ends.\f
HGSS.071.0015:
I will let you know if I find out
anything else.
HGSS.071.0016:
[0103,0000,0000] opened the UNOWN Report
and made notes!
HGSS.071.0017:
Thanks to our research and your
help, we have unraveled so much about\r
their mystery!\f
We have so many things we don’t know
left, of course.\f
Archaeology never ends.
HGSS.071.0018:
There are odd patterns drawn on the
walls of the ruins.\f
They must be the keys for unraveling
the mystery of the ruins.
HGSS.071.0019:
The strange Pokémon you saw in
the ruins?\f
They appear to be very much like the
drawings on the walls there.\f
Hmm...\f
That must mean there are many
different kinds of them...
HGSS.071.0020:
Huh?
I seem to be doing nothing?\f
Come on!
I may not look it, but I’m the one who\r
developed the UNOWN Report!
HGSS.071.0022:
Mystery Pokémon
Name: UNOWN\r
A total of 26 kinds found.
HGSS.071.0023:
Ruins of Alph Research Report\f
I dedicate this report to the
Research Center’s founder,\r
Professor Silktree...
HGSS.071.0024:
There are many academic books.\f
“Mysteries of the Ancients”
“Code-Breaking Guidebook”\r
“Weathering Ruins”
